jokes.text: Charlie makes Frank hang his ass out the window for farting from eating rum ham on a Grilled Charlie
jokes.setup: Frank wants to come in after eating a terrible food combination
jokes.punchline: Charlie making him literally hang his ass out the window until done farting
jokes.explanation: Perfectly encapsulates the show's absurd logic and Charlie's bizarre rules. Frank accepts this as reasonable.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: makes Frank hang his ass out the window for farting from eating rum ham on a Grilled Charlie

jokes.text: Frank: 'If I'm gonna stick my ass out the window, I'm gonna eat.'
jokes.setup: Frank is literally hanging out a window to fart
jokes.punchline: His response is to demand more of the food that caused the problem
jokes.explanation: Frank's commitment to his desires regardless of consequences is peak character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: 'If I'm gonna stick my ass out the window, I'm gonna eat.'

jokes.text: Frank falls out the window with crashing and shattering sounds
jokes.setup: Frank hanging out window for extended period
jokes.punchline: The inevitable physical comedy payoff of him actually falling
jokes.explanation: Classic slapstick setup and payoff, well-earned by the absurd setup

jokes.text: Frank casually drops 'Your mother's dead' in the middle of an argument about chickens
jokes.setup: Normal argument about who came up with contaminating steaks with chickens
jokes.punchline: Frank randomly announces their mother is dead mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection and perfect economy. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Your mother's dead

jokes.text: 'I was just kidding. Your mother's not really dead. I dropped that bomb to soften the blow. We're getting divorced.'
jokes.setup: Frank just announced their mother was dead
jokes.punchline: He was softening the blow for divorce news, which is backwards logic
jokes.explanation: Exceptional structure and logic. Using death to 'soften the blow' of divorce is perfectly backwards Frank logic.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I was just kidding. Your mother's not really dead. I dropped that bomb to soften the blow. We're getting divorced.

jokes.text: 'You're not a funny guy. Don't do bits. They never play.'
jokes.setup: Frank appears to be doing a bit about not knowing them
jokes.punchline: Dennis brutally dismisses Frank's comedic abilities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis arrogance and dismissiveness toward Frank

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You're not a funny guy. Don't do bits. They never play.

jokes.text: Gang sees Frank's bloody head wound and Mac says 'Oh, I see skull!'
jokes.setup: Frank has head injury from falling out window
jokes.punchline: Mac's casual observation of seeing skull
jokes.explanation: Dark visual comedy with Mac's matter-of-fact delivery

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, I see skull!

jokes.text: Concussion test where they get the day wrong, then correct themselves, then Frank questions if he lost a day
jokes.setup: Testing Frank's mental state after head injury
jokes.punchline: They're so incompetent they can't even get the day right for the test
jokes.explanation: Shows the gang's incompetence perfectly - they can't even conduct a basic test

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What day is it?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It's Thursday.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, it's Friday.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's Friday.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Did I lose a day?

jokes.text: 'What is the capital of Pennsylvania?' 'Philadelphia.' 'No.' 'Is it Pittsburgh?' 'At one point, it was Philly.' 'I don't think they change it, Charlie.'
jokes.setup: Trying to test Frank's knowledge
jokes.punchline: They don't know the answer themselves and Charlie thinks capitals change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic that capitals change like sports teams. Shows gang's ignorance.

dialogue.line: What is the capital of Pennsylvania?
Philadelphia.
No.
Is it Pittsburgh?
At one point, it was Philly.
I don't think they change it, Charlie.

jokes.text: 'Who's the President?' 'George Bush.' 'Nah, I was just kidding. The real president is Dick Cheney. He's pulling all the strings.'
jokes.setup: Testing Frank's knowledge of current events
jokes.punchline: Frank's conspiracy theory about Cheney running things
jokes.explanation: Typical Frank conspiracy mindset, but dated political reference

dialogue.line: Who's the President?
George Bush.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Nah, I was just kidding. The real president is Dick Cheney. He's pulling all the strings.

jokes.text: Charlie's theory that Frank went through a time portal window to 2006
jokes.setup: Frank thinks it's 2006 after falling out window
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately jumps to supernatural explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - skipping medical explanation for magical one. Becomes running gag.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Charlie's theory that Frank went through a time portal window to 2006

jokes.text: Mac and Dennis: 'No, they haven't. No, they haven't.' to Charlie saying stranger things have happened
jokes.setup: Charlie claims stranger things have happened than time portals
jokes.punchline: Immediate unified shutdown from Mac and Dennis
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Mac and Dennis as occasional voices of reason

dialogue.speaker: Mac and Dennis
dialogue.line: No, they haven't. No, they haven't.

jokes.text: 'We can't repeat the same mistakes of the past. Keep Charlie away from Frank. He's drawn to him for some inexplicable reason.'
jokes.setup: Planning to exploit Frank's amnesia
jokes.punchline: Dennis accurately identifies Charlie's inexplicable Frank obsession
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on Charlie-Frank dynamic that's true to the show

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We can't repeat the same mistakes of the past. Keep Charlie away from Frank. He's drawn to him for some inexplicable reason.

jokes.text: Dee suggests Wyclef Jean charity scam, then sings 'Gone Till November' incorrectly
jokes.setup: Dee suggests a celebrity charity scam
jokes.punchline: She doesn't actually know the song lyrics while referencing the scam
jokes.explanation: Typical Dee incompetence - references something she doesn't understand

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Dee suggests Wyclef Jean charity scam, then sings 'Gone Till November' incorrectly

jokes.text: 'How do you not have fish?' 'Two can play at that game. Banging your sister is perverted.'
jokes.setup: Frank confused by restaurant not having fish
jokes.punchline: Restaurant response about Dennis and Dee being siblings
jokes.explanation: Random cringe moment suggesting restaurant staff knows about Dennis/Dee weirdness

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: How do you not have fish?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Two can play at that game. Banging your sister is perverted.

jokes.text: Dennis claiming to be working toward becoming a veterinarian, Dee claiming Broadway dreams
jokes.setup: Frank asking about their life progress
jokes.punchline: They lie about having any professional aspirations
jokes.explanation: Shows their fundamental dishonesty and delusion about their lives

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm working toward becoming a veterinarian

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I have Broadway dreams

jokes.text: 'You're what, 25, 26?' 'Yeah.' They're actually almost 40 and working at a bar
jokes.setup: Frank guessing their age
jokes.punchline: They agree despite being much older and exactly what Frank warns against
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - they're exactly what Frank says not to become while pretending they're not

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You're what, 25, 26?
Yeah.

jokes.text: 'There was a disaster in Haiti?' followed by Dee stammering and claiming she invented the phone
jokes.setup: Dee mentions Haiti disaster that hasn't happened in 2006
jokes.punchline: Her terrible cover-up by claiming to have invented smartphones
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from time slip to absurd lie. Pure Dee panic response.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: There was a disaster in Haiti?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I invented the phone.

jokes.text: Dennis and Dee competing over who invented the phone with increasingly ridiculous claims
jokes.setup: Dee claims to have invented smartphones
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately has to one-up her with his own invention claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling rivalry dynamics - neither can let the other have credit

jokes.text: 'This time, you and I are gonna be the ones that bang the strippers.' 'Why do you want to bang them?' 'Cause it's 2006, and you're still into women.'
jokes.setup: Mac planning to succeed with strippers this time
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Charlie's later sexual confusion
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-reference to Charlie's character development. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: This time, you and I are gonna be the ones that bang the strippers.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Why do you want to bang them?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Cause it's 2006, and you're still into women.

jokes.text: Charlie shows Frank his toe knife and explains using it to dig scum out of his toes
jokes.setup: Frank notices a knife in Charlie's bed
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually explains his disgusting toe hygiene routine
jokes.explanation: Iconic Always Sunny moment. Perfectly encapsulates Charlie's bizarre lifestyle as normal to him.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: shows Frank his toe knife and explains using it to dig scum out of his toes

jokes.text: Charlie shouting 'Botched toe!' as Frank considers trying the toe knife
jokes.setup: Frank expresses interest in trying Charlie's toe knife method
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately warns about botched toe, suggesting he's had this injury
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that suggests Charlie's toe knife has caused him injury, delivered with manic energy

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Botched toe!

jokes.text: 'Botched toe! Frank, that is disgusting!' 'Toe knife. Maybe I ought to try that.'
jokes.setup: Stripper is horrified by Charlie's toe knife explanation
jokes.punchline: Frank considers adopting this disgusting practice
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Frank's attraction to Charlie's squalor

dialogue.speaker: Stripper
dialogue.line: Botched toe! Frank, that is disgusting!

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Toe knife. Maybe I ought to try that.

jokes.text: 'He is on a bang path straight to the waitress.'
jokes.setup: Mac realizes Frank is repeating his pattern with strippers
jokes.punchline: The phrase 'bang path' as inevitability toward the waitress
jokes.explanation: Clever phrase that's become quotable. Good economics and wordplay.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: He is on a bang path straight to the waitress.

jokes.text: Dennis's brutal honesty about Dee's acting: 'It's done. You were too old in 2006. You're way too old now. And you're bad at it.'
jokes.setup: Dennis decides to be honest about their futures
jokes.punchline: Completely destroys Dee's dreams with clinical precision
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis cruelty. Methodical destruction of Dee's delusions.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's done. You were too old in 2006. You're way too old now. And you're bad at it.

jokes.text: 'I feel like the only reason you wanted to be a vet was to keep the skins.'
jokes.setup: Dee being honest about Dennis's veterinarian claims
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Dennis's motivation is keeping animal skins
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark insight into Dennis's psychology. Perfectly twisted.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I feel like the only reason you wanted to be a vet was to keep the skins.

jokes.text: You want to keep the skins, don't you? There's no denying the skins are fascinating.
jokes.setup: Dee confronting Dennis about his vet aspirations
jokes.punchline: Dennis admitting his disturbing interest in animal skins
jokes.explanation: Reveals Dennis's psychopathic tendencies in a darkly funny way

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You want to keep the skins, don't you?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: There's no denying the skins are fascinating.

jokes.text: Dee pointing out Dennis is going bald and has back fat, his defensive reaction
jokes.setup: Mutual brutal honesty session
jokes.punchline: Dennis's narcissistic denial of obvious physical flaws
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Dennis's vanity, but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You're going bald and you have back fat.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I am not going bald, and I do not have back fat.

jokes.text: Also you're going bald. I'm going bald?! Where in the hell am I possibly going bald?!
jokes.setup: Dee being brutally honest back to Dennis
jokes.punchline: Dennis's horrified reaction to being told he's balding
jokes.explanation: Dennis's vanity perfectly exposed by his panicked reaction

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Also you're going bald.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm going bald?! Where in the hell am I possibly going bald?!

jokes.text: 'There's no reason why a bald man who enjoys skins who has extra something-something around his belly can't be a goddamn veterinarian!'
jokes.setup: Dennis getting defensive about his flaws
jokes.punchline: His indignant defense while listing all his problems
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with Dennis accepting his flaws while still being delusional

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: There's no reason why a bald man who enjoys skins who has extra something-something around his belly can't be a goddamn veterinarian!

jokes.text: I see fat spilling over the back of your pants on a pretty regular basis
jokes.setup: Dee continuing her brutal honesty
jokes.punchline: Her specific and cruel observation about Dennis's body
jokes.explanation: Dee's revenge cruelty, specific and cutting

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I see fat spilling over the back of your pants on a pretty regular basis

jokes.text: Dennis and Dee wake up with a crack pipe, having smoked crack despite trying to stay clean
jokes.setup: They were trying to reconnect with Frank and stay on track
jokes.punchline: They immediately fell back into crack addiction
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing their complete lack of self-control

dialogue.line: Dennis and Dee wake up with a crack pipe, having smoked crack despite trying to stay clean

jokes.text: 'We'll get back on crack. I mean, on track. Unless you have more crack.'
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to be positive about their relapse
jokes.punchline: Freudian slip revealing his true priorities
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing Dennis's addiction taking over his speech

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We'll get back on crack. I mean, on track. Unless you have more crack.

jokes.text: 'No one in the history of crack has ever woken up in the morning with more crack.'
jokes.setup: Dennis asking if she has more crack
jokes.punchline: Dee's matter-of-fact observation about crack addiction
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark observational humor with perfect economy

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: No one in the history of crack has ever woken up in the morning with more crack.

jokes.text: 'I'm a recovering crackhead, and this is my donkey-brained sister that I take care of.'
jokes.setup: Dennis and Dee trying to get welfare
jokes.punchline: Dennis's casual description of Dee as donkey-brained
jokes.explanation: Good callback to running gag with Dennis's matter-of-fact cruelty

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm a recovering crackhead, and this is my donkey-brained sister that I take care of.

jokes.text: Forged document where they crossed out 'Frank' and wrote 'Dee' and crossed out 'not' from 'not donkey brained'
jokes.setup: They need proof of Dee's mental state
jokes.punchline: Obvious forgery of Frank's certificate
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing their incompetence at fraud

dialogue.line: Forged document where they crossed out 'Frank' and wrote 'Dee' and crossed out 'not' from 'not donkey brained'

jokes.text: Welfare worker's dismissive 'I'm done with this bullshit' and easy approval process
jokes.setup: Dennis and Dee's obvious scam attempt
jokes.punchline: System doesn't care and approves them anyway
jokes.explanation: Social commentary on bureaucratic indifference

dialogue.speaker: Welfare Worker
dialogue.line: I'm done with this bullshit

jokes.text: 'Obama just gives you the money; it's great.' while they're both crackheads trying to scam welfare
jokes.setup: Dennis praising the easy welfare system
jokes.punchline: They're exactly the kind of people who abuse it
jokes.explanation: Political humor showing their self-serving perspective

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Obama just gives you the money; it's great.

jokes.text: All the gang ending up at the welfare store, Charlie still insisting it's 2006
jokes.setup: They all had plans to improve their lives
jokes.punchline: They all ended up in the same place they started
jokes.explanation: Good structural joke showing their inevitable failure

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: All the gang ending up at the welfare store, Charlie still insisting it's 2006

jokes.text: Frank's lawyer introducing herself and Frank calling her 'toots' then cackling
jokes.setup: They meet with Frank's money manager
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately disrespects her with sexist language and inappropriate laughter
jokes.explanation: Standard Frank inappropriate behavior - shows his casual sexism from the 2006 era

jokes.text: 'Mr. Doran? He passed away.' Frank laughing and calling for Jerry to stop the bit
jokes.setup: Frank asking for his old lawyer
jokes.punchline: Frank doesn't believe his lawyer is actually dead
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about Frank's disconnect from reality

dialogue.speaker: Lawyer
dialogue.line: Mr. Doran? He passed away.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Frank laughing and calling for Jerry to stop the bit

jokes.text: 'You goddamn bitch! You wouldn't know a good idea if it smacked you in a tit!'
jokes.setup: Lawyer dismisses the phone invention claim
jokes.punchline: Frank's misogynistic outburst
jokes.explanation: Typical Frank crudeness when his schemes fail

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You goddamn bitch! You wouldn't know a good idea if it smacked you in a tit!

jokes.text: You goddamn bitch! You wouldn't know a good idea if it smacked you in a tit!
jokes.setup: Lauren rejecting their phone 'invention'
jokes.punchline: Dennis's explosive misogynistic outburst
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's true character when his schemes fail

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You goddamn bitch! You wouldn't know a good idea if it smacked you in a tit!

jokes.text: Frank writes them a check but it's dated 2006, making it invalid
jokes.setup: Frank finally writes them the money
jokes.punchline: His time confusion makes the check worthless
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic payoff to the entire scheme. Great structure.

dialogue.line: Frank writes them a check but it's dated 2006, making it invalid

jokes.text: Charlie's roommate interviews including cat food questions and Frank saying he'd always eat it
jokes.setup: Normal roommate interview questions
jokes.punchline: Frank's commitment to eating cat food in all scenarios
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank logic and callback to Charlie's eating habits

jokes.text: Charlie trying to throw the waitress out the window to send her back to 2006
jokes.setup: Waitress won't live with Charlie
jokes.punchline: Charlie's solution is to throw her through the 'time portal'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to Charlie's time portal theory with physical escalation

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Charlie trying to throw the waitress out the window to send her back to 2006

jokes.text: Frank asking 'We having a gang bang?' when he sees everyone in Charlie's apartment
jokes.setup: Frank walks into crowded apartment
jokes.punchline: His immediate assumption is sexual
jokes.explanation: Typical Frank crudeness but economical and character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: We having a gang bang?

jokes.text: Bank teller can't cash the check because it's dated 2006 - final confirmation they're back to normal
jokes.setup: Dennis and Dee try to cash Frank's check
jokes.punchline: The 2006 date makes it worthless, proving the time confusion is over
jokes.explanation: Good structural payoff that confirms the return to normalcy

dialogue.speaker: Bank Teller
dialogue.line: can't cash the check because it's dated 2006
